name: Corbado Passkey Authentication API
what_it_does: Passkey-first authentication platform with REST API for adding FIDO2/WebAuthn passkey login to any application — handling credential registration, assertion verification, user management, and passkey lifecycle with support for cross-device passkeys and fallback authentication.
use_cases: Adding passkey registration and login to web and mobile apps via REST API
Managing user passkey credentials (list, revoke, audit) for security dashboard
Implementing passkey-as-a-service without building WebAuthn infrastructure from scratch
Migrating existing password users to passkeys with gradual rollout via API control
Verifying passkey assertions in custom server-side authentication flows
not_for: Full-featured auth platform with RBAC, organizations, and SSO (use Auth0 or Kinde)
Teams needing on-premise deployment of passkey infrastructure
Consumer apps needing full identity management beyond passkeys
best_when: A developer needs to add passkey authentication quickly without implementing WebAuthn from scratch, with API control over credential lifecycle.
avoid_when: You need full identity management, RBAC, SSO, or a complete auth platform — Corbado specializes in passkeys specifically.

auth.notes: Project API key for server-side calls. Short-lived session tokens for client-side passkey flows. Project ID + API secret pair. No OAuth2 scopes — project-level access control.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: up to 1,000 MAU, unlimited passkeys
pricing.notes: Free tier generous for most startups. German company (Hamburg) with strong EU compliance culture. SOC2 in progress. Focus exclusively on passkeys — not trying to be full auth platform. Good for passkey-first products.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per plan; free tier has lower limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API covers passkey registration initiation, assertion verification, user management, and credential management. SDKs for 6 languages simplify integration. Webhook support for passkey events. Well-documented flows with sequence diagrams.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: User creation idempotent by identifier. Passkey registration initiates new challenge each time — not idempotent. Credential deletion idempotent. User queries idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Domain verification required — relying party ID must match app domain
Passkey registration requires browser/device interaction — cannot be automated without user
Challenge expiry (60s default) — must complete ceremony within window
Cross-device passkeys (QR code flow) require additional UI handling

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. FIDO2/WebAuthn by design — phishing-resistant. GDPR compliant. EU data residency available. Domain binding prevents credential theft. API secrets server-side only. No password storage — pure passkeys. German company with strong EU privacy culture.
